mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity prices and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive-return hit rate falls below 50% when FEDFUNDS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 3-month low (a Sahm-rule style trigger), it signals an incipient recession that investors have not yet fully priced, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of latest snapshot, near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals a broad-based consumer and housing demand contraction that single-series readings understate, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because neither series alone reliably predicts equity drawdowns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline concurrently
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST decline signal produces forward 20d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During an expansionary regime (UNRATE below 4.5% and FEDFUNDS not in a tightening trend), positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals continued industrial output momentum consistent with rising corporate earnings, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.23 with UNRATE at 3.9% indicating expansion
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM readings in low-UNRATE regimes fail to produce mean 5d forward SPX return greater than unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in resilient demand.
failure_condition: Mean forward 5d SPX return following positive PAYEMS prints is not significantly different from mean return following negative prints (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 4%, markets anticipate continued restrictive monetary policy from the Federal Reserve, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation shows sustained deceleration.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns during periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime characterized by elevated FEDFUNDS relative to its 2-year trailing average, a month-over-month decline in HOUST signals housing-sector stress transmitting to broader credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the effect compounds through consumer wealth channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline while FEDFUNDS above its trailing 24-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1168k units, moderating from prior highs
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following HOUST declines in high-rate regimes do not differ significantly from returns following HOUST declines in low-rate regimes (interaction effect p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of falling UNRATE alongside rising FEDFUNDS indicates the Federal Reserve is tightening into labor market strength (an overheating-response regime), which historically precedes policy-induced growth slowdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, an effect not captured by either series individually.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE trailing 6-month decline AND FEDFUNDS trailing 6-month increase
regime_hint: monetary tightening into tight labor market
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-falling/FEDFUNDS-rising signal shows no incremental Rank IC over the single-series FEDFUNDS trend signal, or SPX forward returns in triggered periods exceed baseline unconditional returns.
